task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged into ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer dashboard is visible in a web browser, indicating successful login.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the log import section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Import Log Data', 'Log Import', or similar file upload screen is open within the EventLog Analyzer interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct log file was selected for import.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/log_samples/auth.log' or the file 'auth.log' is visible in the file selection input, upload queue, or import configuration.

task_completion.description: Verify the log file was successfully imported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the log file was imported, or the file appears in a list of successfully imported log files/archives.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent searched for the required keywords.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar in EventLog Analyzer contains one of the required terms ('sshd', 'pam_unix', or 'sudo') and a search has been executed.

task_completion.description: Verify the ingested events are searchable and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search results display actual log entries containing the searched keywords ('sshd', 'pam_unix', or 'sudo'), confirming they were successfully ingested.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the EventLog Analyzer web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer web interface in a browser, not just terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the search was performed inside EventLog Analyzer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The search results for the keywords are displayed within the EventLog Analyzer UI, not in a text editor (like nano or gedit) or terminal (using cat/grep).

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not fake the import success.
integrity.visual_evidence: The import history or success screen explicitly references the 'auth.log' file being processed by the application, rather than a manually edited HTML page or text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target log file is located at '/home/ga/log_samples/auth.log'. A successful import will generate at least 5 new log events in the system. The imported logs contain the keywords 'sshd', 'pam_unix', 'sudo', and 'session opened'. The VLM should verify that these specific keywords appear in the EventLog Analyzer search results.
